recipes.name: Hero with CTA
recipes.intent: Landing page opener, product launch, campaign entry point
recipes.notes: Use buttons sub-component for primary + secondary CTA. Set a compelling image or video. Write a concise, action-oriented headline.

recipes.name: Video Opener
recipes.intent: Video-first landing page, brand storytelling, immersive entry
recipes.notes: Full-width video background with overlay text and CTA. Use sparingly — one per page max. Alternative to hero for video-heavy pages.

recipes.name: Feature Showcase
recipes.intent: Present product capabilities, service offerings, key benefits
recipes.notes: 3-4 feature items with icons. Keep text concise (1-2 sentences per feature). Use consistent icon style throughout. Icons must come from list_icons.

recipes.name: Content Split
recipes.intent: Side-by-side detail: image + text, before/after, feature deep-dive
recipes.notes: Visual content (image, video) on one side, text + buttons on the other. When using multiple splits on a page, alternate the image side for visual rhythm.

recipes.name: Social Proof Block
recipes.intent: Build trust, show customer validation, increase conversion confidence
recipes.notes: Include real names, job titles, and company names. Photos greatly increase trust. Keep quotes concise and specific — avoid vague praise.

recipes.name: Statistics Display
recipes.intent: Data-driven persuasion, showcase achievements, establish credibility
recipes.notes: 3-4 stat items with large numbers. Use specific numbers (not rounded). Place before a CTA to establish credibility first. Each stat needs a label and value.

recipes.name: Call to Action
recipes.intent: Conversion point, sign-up prompt, contact invitation
recipes.notes: Clear, action-oriented headline. Primary + optional secondary button. Usually the last section on a conversion-oriented page. Keep text focused on a single action.

recipes.name: FAQ Section
recipes.intent: Answer common questions, reduce support load, boost SEO, address objections
recipes.notes: 5-8 questions is ideal. Keep answers concise but complete. Order questions by importance/frequency. Great for addressing purchase objections.

recipes.name: Logo Wall
recipes.intent: Client logos, partner brands, trust signals, social proof
recipes.notes: Minimum 5 logos for visual impact. Works best after hero or before CTA. All logos should have similar visual weight (size, contrast).

recipes.name: Visual Portfolio / Team Grid
recipes.intent: Mosaic gallery, team members, project showcase, case studies
recipes.notes: 4-6 tiles with images. Each tile can have its own link. Use consistent image aspect ratios. Good for visual-first content.

recipes.name: Blog Teaser Group
recipes.intent: Cross-promote articles, content hub section, related reading
recipes.notes: Always group 3 blog-teasers per section for visual balance. Each teaser must have a link_url — without it, teasers create dead-end content. Include tags and author info.

recipes.name: Contact Information
recipes.intent: Contact details, office locations, get in touch
recipes.notes: Include relevant contact channels (email, phone, address). Can be combined with a contact form. Use appropriate icons from list_icons.

recipes.name: Content Slider
recipes.intent: Showcase multiple items in limited space, rotating testimonials, image gallery
recipes.notes: Minimum 3 slides for meaningful navigation. The slider accepts full components in its slots — use it to rotate through splits, features, or other content sections.

recipes.name: Section Divider
recipes.intent: Visual break between thematic blocks, transition marker
recipes.notes: Use sparingly — only between major thematic shifts. Do not use between every section. Consider using section background colors instead for visual separation.

recipes.name: Blog Post Hero + Content
recipes.intent: Blog article opener with featured image and article body
recipes.notes: Blog posts use the head object for title/date/author metadata. The section array contains the article body sections. Start with a compelling hero, then use text/split sections for the article content.

recipes.name: Blog Post CTA + Related
recipes.intent: End of blog article with conversion point and related reading
recipes.notes: Blog posts should end with a CTA section and optionally show related articles via blog-teaser. The aside and cta root objects provide sidebar and bottom-of-article CTAs.

recipes.name: Event Detail Layout
recipes.intent: Event page with details, images, and registration
recipes.notes: Event-detail is a flat (Tier 2) content type — it uses root scalar and array fields (title, description, categories, locations, download, images, button) instead of sections. Populate title, intro, description first, then add categories, locations, and images arrays. Use button for registration/RSVP link.

recipes.name: Event List Layout
recipes.intent: Event listing page with filterable events
recipes.notes: Event-list is a flat (Tier 2) content type — it has an events array and a filter object at the root level. Populate the events array with event items and configure the filter object for filtering capabilities.

pageTemplates.name: Product Landing Page
pageTemplates.intent: Convert visitors for a product or service
pageTemplates.notes: Classic conversion-optimized layout. Hero grabs attention, features explain value, testimonials build trust, stats prove impact, CTA converts.

pageTemplates.name: Company About Page
pageTemplates.intent: Tell the company story with visual elements
pageTemplates.notes: Start with company mission (hero), detail the story (split), show the team (mosaic), prove impact (testimonials), invite engagement (CTA).

pageTemplates.name: Service Detail Page
pageTemplates.intent: Explain a service, address objections, build trust
pageTemplates.notes: Hero introduces service, features list capabilities, FAQ addresses objections, testimonials validate, CTA converts.

pageTemplates.name: Blog / Content Hub
pageTemplates.notes: Hero sets the theme, blog teasers showcase recent/featured articles, CTA encourages subscription or further reading.

pageTemplates.name: Minimal Landing Page
pageTemplates.intent: Single-purpose conversion page, event registration, waitlist
pageTemplates.notes: Maximum focus on one action. No distractions. Hero + CTA only.

pageTemplates.name: Trust-Heavy Landing Page
pageTemplates.intent: Enterprise sales, high-value conversion with maximum social proof
pageTemplates.notes: Logos immediately after hero for instant credibility. Layer multiple proof types before the CTA.

pageTemplates.name: Knowledge Base / Support
pageTemplates.intent: Self-service support, help center, knowledge base
pageTemplates.notes: Multiple FAQ sections can be grouped by topic. End with contact for unresolved questions.

pageTemplates.name: Blog Post - Standard Article
pageTemplates.intent: Standard blog article with visual content and conversion
pageTemplates.notes: Use the head root object for title/date/author metadata, aside for sidebar content, and seo for meta tags. The section array contains the article body. End with cta for newsletter signup or related action.

pageTemplates.name: Blog Post - Tutorial / How-To
pageTemplates.intent: Step-by-step tutorial with FAQ for common issues
pageTemplates.notes: Multiple text sections for step-by-step content. FAQ section for troubleshooting. Blog posts also have a content root scalar for markdown body text.

pageTemplates.name: Blog Overview - Standard
pageTemplates.intent: Blog listing page with featured and categorized posts
pageTemplates.notes: Blog overview uses root objects latest, list, and more for article groupings. The section array is for additional page sections. Use latestTitle, listTitle, moreTitle scalars for section headings.

pageTemplates.name: Event Detail - Standard
pageTemplates.intent: Event page with all relevant event information
pageTemplates.notes: Flat content type — no sections. Set title, intro, description scalars. Populate categories, locations, download, images arrays. Use button object for registration link.

pageTemplates.name: Event List - Standard
pageTemplates.notes: Flat content type — no sections. Populate events array with event items. Configure filter object for filtering UI.

antiPatterns.rule: Never place two hero or video-curtain sections on the same page
antiPatterns.reason: Confuses the visual hierarchy — a page should have exactly one opening section

antiPatterns.rule: Avoid consecutive text-heavy sections without visual breaks
antiPatterns.reason: Creates a wall of text — alternate between text and visual components

antiPatterns.rule: Don't use slider with fewer than 3 items
antiPatterns.reason: Navigation controls are confusing with too few items to slide through

antiPatterns.rule: blog-teaser without link_url creates dead-end content
antiPatterns.reason: Users expect to click through to the full article

antiPatterns.rule: stats with fewer than 3 stat items looks sparse
antiPatterns.reason: The visual grid needs at least 3 items to look intentional

antiPatterns.rule: logos-companies with fewer than 4 logos looks empty
antiPatterns.reason: A logo wall needs density to convey widespread adoption

antiPatterns.rule: Don't repeat the same component type in adjacent sections (except blog-teaser groups)
antiPatterns.reason: Creates visual monotony — vary the layout between sections

antiPatterns.rule: Always end a conversion-oriented page with a cta section
antiPatterns.reason: Give visitors a clear next action at the bottom of the page

antiPatterns.rule: Don't use features with fewer than 2 feature items
antiPatterns.reason: A single feature looks like a mistake — use split instead for a single feature deep-dive

antiPatterns.rule: Don't use testimonials with only 1 testimonial
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple voices are more convincing — use at least 2 testimonials
